name: Viking 2 lander
fact: Operated 1,281 days at 48°N — the highest-latitude Mars surface mission until Phoenix. Captured the first frost on Mars (winter 1979). The lander's biological experiments returned the same ambiguous results as Viking 1's: chemistry that mimicked metabolism, but without unambiguous biology.
capability: Demonstrated Mars surface operations could survive multi-year northern-hemisphere seasonal cycles.
